diagrams.title: Control Unit Architecture Overview
diagrams.description: This diagram provides a side-by-side comparison of Hardwired and Microprogrammed Control Unit architectures, showing the fundamental structural differences between the two approaches. It helps students visualize the overall organization and understand the key distinction: hardware-based vs firmware-based control.
elements: Hardwired Control Unit block with combinational logic
Microprogrammed Control Unit block with Control Memory
Instruction Register (IR) input to both units
Control signals output from both units
diagrams.purpose: Explain the two fundamental approaches to implementing control units in CPU design and their structural differences

diagrams.title: Hardwired Control Unit Internal Structure
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the internal structure of a Hardwired Control Unit, displaying how control signals are generated through combinational logic circuits. It includes the instruction decoder, state machine/sequencer, control logic gates, and the generation of control signals for datapath components.
elements: Control Signal Outputs (to ALU, Registers, Memory)
diagrams.purpose: Explain how Hardwired Control uses combinational logic and state machines to generate control signals directly from instructions

diagrams.title: Microprogrammed Control Unit Internal Structure
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the internal structure of a Microprogrammed Control Unit, showing the control memory (ROM), microprogram counter, microinstruction register, and the process of fetching and executing microinstructions. It demonstrates the two-level control approach: macro-instructions interpreted by microcode.
diagrams.purpose: Explain how Microprogrammed Control uses stored microinstructions to generate control signals, showing the fetch-execute cycle at microcode level
